rulings.Case Title: SANTIAGO ROBLETO VS. JULIA VARGAS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For An Order Deeming That All Plaintiffs' Objections To Defendant Julia Vargas'S Request For Productions Of Documents Are Deemed Waived & For Monetary Sanctions, Issue Sanctions, & Evidentiary Sanctions; Or, In The Alternative, To Compel Further And Proper Responses To Defendant Julia Vargas'S Request For Production Of Documents & For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 7, 2013, Line 1: Defendant JULIA VARGAS' Motion for an Order Deeming that All Plaintiffs' Objections to Defendant Julia Vargas's Request for Productions of Documents are Deemed Waived & for Monetary Sanctions, Issue Sanctions, & Evidentiary Sanctions; or, in the Alternative, to Compel Further and Proper Responses to Defendant Julia Vargas's Request for Production of Documents & for Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Jeffrey Windsor, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: VARGAS¿ motion to compel further and proper responses to VARGAS¿ Form Interrogatories ¿ Set One is GRANTED, with the exception of Form Interrogatories 7.1 and 12.4. Plaintiffs shall provide further responses which comply with Code of Civil Procedure (¿C.C.P.¿) sections 2030.210 et seq. within fifteen (15) days of the date of this order. VARGAS¿ motion to compel further and proper responses to VARGAS¿ First Request for Production of Documents ¿ Set One is GRANTED. Plaintiffs shall provide further responses which comply with C.C.P. sections 2031.210 et seq. within fifteen (15) days of the date of this order. VARGAS¿ motion to compel further and proper responses to VARGAS¿ Requests for Admissions ¿ Set Two is GRANTED, with the exception of Requests for Admissions 14, 16, and 17. Plaintiffs shall provide further responses which comply with C.C.P. sections 2033.210 et seq. within fifteen (15) days of the date of this order. Plaintiffs and/or any one of them and/or their counsel, NICHOLAS DAMER, shall pay sanctions to VARGAS, through her attorneys of record, in the amount of $2,500, no later than fifteen (15) days from the date of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jeff@windsor-plc.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ESTATE OF FRANKLIN GOLDBERG AND JUNA GOLDBERG VS. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Omton To Strike And Tax Costs And Objections To Defts Memo Of P&A
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, August 7, 2013, Line 1, PLAINTIFF ESTATE OF FRANKLIN GOLDBERG AND JUNA GOLDBERG Notice Of Motion And Omton To Strike And Tax Costs And Objections To Defts Memo Of P&A The motion is transferred to department 504 to be heard before Judge Busch at 1:30 p.m. on September 13, 2013, with the motion for new trial. Judge Busch is the judge that heard the trial in this matter. Judge Busch asks that Defendants file a response to the argument raised by plaintiffs in Section IV of their reply brief on the motion to tax costs that 998 costs should not be available because the offer was a token offer. The Defendants' response should be filed nine court days prior to the September 13 hearing date. The court will not provide a court reporter for this hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =302/mjm

rulings.Case Title: NETSANET HAILEMICHAEL VS. BANK OF THE WEST, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Re Subpoenas And Award Of Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, August 7, 2013, Line 15, PLAINTIFF NETSANET HAILEMICHAEL Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Re Subpoenas And Award Of Monetary Sanctions The motion granted in part. The subpoenas shall be modified and issued pursuant to the Court's order. The subpoenas to Dr. Hseuh, The Mayo Clinic, Kaiser, and Dr. Hothi DDS are overbroad in seeking "any and all" records without restriction as to type of medical condition, scope or time under Britt v. Superior Court (1978) 20 Cal.3d 844 and its progeny. The Court orders that subpoenas to these providers be limited to records pertaining to plaintiff's ulcer, and limited in time to three years before filing of this lawsuit, i.e., it may seek records from February 2010 to present. The subpoena to psychologist Dr. Smith shall be limited to three years before filing of this lawsuit, i.e., it may seek records from February 2010 to present. The employment records subpoena to Bank of America shall be limited in time to records from plaintiff's last year of employment there, and limited in subject matter to her keyholder's privileges there. Plaintiff's request for a "first look" at records produced is denied. Sanctions are denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/mjm
